perturbation.original_text: NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project.
perturbation.changed_text: NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly interfere with each other's interest with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid payment of established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate' and 'directly or indirectly payment', it now implies only direct interference is prohibited. Indirect interference would be allowable, therefore, making the remedy in section VI less enforceable if a party interferes indirectly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VI. REMEDIES - The p arties herein acknowledge that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the injured party shall be entitled to injunctive relief as a cumulative and not necessarily successive remedy and without the need to post bond. Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Disclosing Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief. If any term, provision, covenant, or condition of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or a regulatory agency having jurisdiction over the subject matter herein to be enforceable, void or invalid, the remainder of the provisions herein shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidates an d the rights and obligations of the parties of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited both direct and indirect interference, while the changed text only prohibits direct interference. This creates a contradiction with Section VI, which provides remedies for a 'breach of this Agreement'. If only direct interference is prohibited, then indirect interference is allowed, and the remedies outlined in Section VI would be less enforceable in cases of indirect interference, as indicated in the perturbation explanation. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly or indirectly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement. No contract or agreement providing for any transaction involving the Parties shall be deemed to exist between the Parties unless and until a final definit ive agreement has been executed and delivered. Nothing in this Agreement shall constitute or be deemed to constitute a partnership between the parties hereto or constitute or be deemed to constitute one party as agent of the other, for any purpose whatsoev er, and neither party shall have the authority or power to bind the other, or to contract in the name of or create a liability against the other, in any way or for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement. No contract or agreement providing for any transaction involving the Parties shall be deemed to exist between the Parties unless and until a final definit ive agreement has been executed and delivered. Nothing in this Agreement shall constitute or be deemed to constitute a partnership between the parties hereto or constitute or be deemed to constitute one party as agent of the other, for any purpose whatsoev er, and neither party shall have the authority or power to bind the other, or to contract in the name of or create a liability against the other, in any way or for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'or indirectly' it implies that Trade Secrets or Confidential Information can be transfered indirectly now. Because of this indirect transfers are now allowed, but section VI still refers to remedy for a breach. It can be argued the breach is not enforceable since indirect transfer is now legal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VI. REMEDIES - The p arties herein acknowledge that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the injured party shall be entitled to injunctive relief as a cumulative and not necessarily successive remedy and without the need to post bond. Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Disclosing Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief. If any term, provision, covenant, or condition of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or a regulatory agency having jurisdiction over the subject matter herein to be enforceable, void or invalid, the remainder of the provisions herein shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidates an d the rights and obligations of the parties of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'or indirectly', meaning that Trade Secrets or Confidential Information can be transferred indirectly. This conflicts with Section VI, which outlines remedies for 'a breach of this Agreement'. If indirect transfer is now permissible, it creates a contradiction with the remedy clause for breaches, as it would be unclear whether indirect transfer is a breach that can be enforced, as stated in the explanation. The explanation suggests that indirect transfer is now legal and therefore can't be enforced. Removing a term in the original text causes a contradiction with the contradicted section which expects it to be covered under possible breaches.

perturbation.original_text: TRADE SECRET (a) The undersigned and/or their employees, agents, affiliates, attorneys, accountants, and advisors acknowledge that all information shared while performing duties related to the development of potential acquisition of business or any customers introduced whether in written, printed, verbal or electronic forms by eith er party, including but not limited to files, employees, clients, suppliers, names and addresses, products samples, designs and patents, copyrights, manufacturing processes, sales and marketing projects and programs are to be held up as proprietary informa tion and a trade secret of the "Disclosing Party" and as such are to be treated in a strictly confidential manner.
perturbation.changed_text: TRADE SECRET (a) The undersigned and/or their employees, agents, affiliates, attorneys, accountants, and advisors acknowledge that only client names shared while performing duties related to the development of potential acquisition of business or any customers introduced whether in written, printed, verbal or electronic forms by eith er party, including but not limited to files, employees, clients, suppliers, names and addresses, products samples, designs and patents, copyrights, manufacturing processes, sales and marketing projects and programs are to be held up as proprietary informa tion and a trade secret of the "Disclosing Party" and as such are to be treated in a strictly confidential manner.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "all information shared" to "only client names shared", this significantly narrows the scope of what constitutes a Trade Secret. This will make the term trade secret contradict what was define in the confidential information in section I.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - As used herein, Confidential Information shall mean any information and data of a confidential or proprietary nature which is disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, limited to customer lists and contact information only, directly exchanged between the parties. The terms and existence of this Agreement, the fact that Confidential Information has been made av ailable hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a potential business relationship involving the Parties and all the terms, conditions, and other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof) shall also be conside red Confidential Information that is subject to the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Trade Secret' to 'only client names shared,' while the contradicted text (Section I. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION) defines Confidential Information as being 'limited to customer lists and contact information only, directly exchanged between the parties.' The change from 'all information shared' to 'only client names shared' in the context of defining 'Trade Secret' directly conflicts with the broader definition of 'Confidential Information' that includes 'customer lists and contact information.' This contradiction creates uncertainty about which information is considered a trade secret and confidential information, leading to potential disputes. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
